NKJV.1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread drew near, which is called Passover.
NKJV.2: And the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might kill Him, for they feared the people.
NKJV.3: Then Satan entered Judas, surnamed Iscariot, who was numbered among the twelve.
NKJV.4: So he went his way and conferred with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray Him to them.
NKJV.5: And they were glad, and agreed to give him money.
NKJV.6: So he promised and sought opportunity to betray Him to them in the absence of the multitude.
NKJV.7: Then came the Day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover must be killed.
NKJV.8: And He sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat.”
NKJV.9: So they said to Him, “Where do You want us to prepare?”
NKJV.10: And He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered the city, a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him into the house which he enters.
NKJV.11: Then you shall say to the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with My disciples?” ’
NKJV.12: Then he will show you a large, furnished upper room; there make ready.”
NKJV.13: So they went and found it just as He had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.
NKJV.14: When the hour had come, He sat down, and the twelve apostles with Him.
NKJV.15: Then He said to them, “With fervent desire I have desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;
NKJV.16: for I say to you, I will no longer eat of it  until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.17: Then He took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves;
NKJV.18: for  I say to you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
NKJV.19: And He took bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, “This is My body which is given for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”
NKJV.20: Likewise He also took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is shed for you.
NKJV.21: But behold, the hand of My betrayer is with Me on the table.
NKJV.22: And truly the Son of Man goes  as it has been determined, but woe to that man by whom He is betrayed!”
NKJV.23: Then they began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.
NKJV.24: Now there was also a dispute among them, as to which of them should be considered the greatest.
NKJV.25: And He said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them, and those who exercise authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’
NKJV.26: But not so among you; on the contrary, he who is greatest among you, let him be as the younger, and he who governs as he who serves.
NKJV.27: For who is greater, he who sits at the table, or he who serves? Is it not he who sits at the table? Yet I am among you as the One who serves.
NKJV.28: “But you are those who have continued with Me in  My trials.
NKJV.29: And I bestow upon you a kingdom, just as My Father bestowed one upon Me,
NKJV.30: that  you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom,  and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”
NKJV.31: And the Lord said, “Simon, Simon! Indeed, Satan has asked for you, that he may sift you as wheat.
NKJV.32: But I have prayed for you, that your faith should not fail; and when you have returned to Me, strengthen your brethren.”
NKJV.33: But he said to Him, “Lord, I am ready to go with You, both to prison and to death.”
NKJV.34: Then He said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster shall not crow this day before you will deny three times that you know Me.”
NKJV.35: And He said to them, “When I sent you without money bag, knapsack, and sandals, did you lack anything?” So they said, “Nothing.”
NKJV.36: Then He said to them, “But now, he who has a money bag, let him take it, and likewise a knapsack; and he who has no sword, let him sell his garment and buy one.
NKJV.37: For I say to you that this which is written must still be accomplished in Me:  ‘And He was numbered with the transgressors.’ For the things concerning Me have an end.”
NKJV.38: So they said, “Lord, look, here are two swords.” And He said to them, “It is enough.”
NKJV.39: Coming out, He went to the Mount of Olives, as He was accustomed, and His disciples also followed Him.
NKJV.40: When He came to the place, He said to them, “Pray that you may not enter into temptation.”
NKJV.41: And He was withdrawn from them about a stone’s throw, and He knelt down and prayed,
NKJV.42: saying, “Father, if it is Your will, take this cup away from Me; nevertheless  not My will, but Yours, be done.”
NKJV.43: Then an angel appeared to Him from heaven, strengthening Him.
NKJV.44: And being in agony, He prayed more earnestly. Then His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
NKJV.45: When He rose up from prayer, and had come to His disciples, He found them sleeping from sorrow.
NKJV.46: Then He said to them, “Why  do you sleep? Rise and  pray, lest you enter into temptation.”
NKJV.47: And while He was still speaking, behold, a multitude; and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them and drew near to Jesus to kiss Him.
NKJV.48: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a  kiss?”
NKJV.49: When those around Him saw what was going to happen, they said to Him, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”
NKJV.50: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
NKJV.51: But Jesus answered and said, “Permit even this.” And He touched his ear and healed him.
NKJV.52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and the elders who had come to Him, “Have you come out, as against a  robber, with swords and clubs?
NKJV.53: When I was with you daily in the  temple, you did not try to seize Me. But this is your  hour, and the power of darkness.”
NKJV.54: Having arrested Him, they led Him and brought Him into the high priest’s house. But Peter followed at a distance.
NKJV.55: Now when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
NKJV.56: And a certain servant girl, seeing him as he sat by the fire, looked intently at him and said, “This man was also with Him.”
NKJV.57: But he denied Him, saying, “Woman, I do not know Him.”
NKJV.58: And after a little while another saw him and said, “You also are of them.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
NKJV.59: Then after about an hour had passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, “Surely this fellow also was with Him, for he is a Galilean.”
NKJV.60: But Peter said, “Man, I do not know what you are saying!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
NKJV.61: And the Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how He had said to him, “Before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.”

NKJV.63: Now the men who held Jesus mocked Him and beat Him.
NKJV.64: And having blindfolded Him, they struck Him on the face and asked Him, saying, “Prophesy! Who is the one who struck You?”
NKJV.65: And many other things they blasphemously spoke against Him.
NKJV.66: As soon as it was day, the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, came together and led Him into their council, saying,
NKJV.67: “If You are the Christ, tell us.” But He said to them, “If I tell you, you will  by no means believe.
NKJV.68: And if I also ask you, you will by no means answer Me or let Me go.
NKJV.69: Hereafter the Son of Man will sit on the right hand of the power of God.”
NKJV.70: Then they all said, “Are You then the Son of God?” So He said to them, “You rightly say that I am.”
NKJV.71: And they said, “What further testimony do we need? For we have heard it ourselves from His own mouth.”

ESV.1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread drew near, which is called the Passover.
ESV.2: And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how to put him to death, for they feared the people.
ESV.3: Then Satan entered into Judas called Iscariot, who was of the number of the twelve.
ESV.4: He went away and conferred with the chief priests and officers how he might betray him to them.
ESV.5: And they were glad, and agreed to give him money.
ESV.6: So he consented and sought an opportunity to betray him to them in the absence of a crowd.
ESV.7: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread, on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
ESV.8: So Jesus  sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat it.”
ESV.9: They said to him, “Where will you have us prepare it?”
ESV.10: He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him into the house that he enters
ESV.11: and tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?’
ESV.12: And he will show you a large upper room furnished; prepare it there.”
ESV.13: And they went and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
ESV.14: And when the hour came, he reclined at table, and the apostles with him.
ESV.15: And he said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
ESV.16: For I tell you I will not eat it  until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
ESV.17: And he took a cup, and when he had given thanks he said, “Take this, and divide it among yourselves.
ESV.18: For I tell you that from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
ESV.19: And he took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying,  “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
ESV.20: And likewise the cup after they had eaten, saying,  “This cup that is poured out for you is the new covenant in my blood.
ESV.21: But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.
ESV.22: For the Son of Man goes as it has been determined, but woe to that man by whom he is betrayed!”
ESV.23: And they began to question one another, which of them it could be who was going to do this.
ESV.24: A dispute also arose among them, as to which of them was to be regarded as the greatest.
ESV.25: And he said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them, and those in authority over them are called benefactors.
ESV.26: But not so with you. Rather, let the greatest among you become as the youngest, and the leader as one who serves.
ESV.27: For who is the greater, one who reclines at table or one who serves? Is it not the one who reclines at table? But I am among you as the one who serves.
ESV.28: “You are those who have stayed with me in my trials,
ESV.29: and I assign to you, as my Father assigned to me, a kingdom,
ESV.30: that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
ESV.31: “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan demanded to have you,  that he might sift you like wheat,
ESV.32: but I have prayed for you that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned again, strengthen your brothers.”
ESV.33: Peter  said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death.”
ESV.34: Jesus  said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will not crow this day, until you deny three times that you know me.”
ESV.35: And he said to them,  “When I sent you out with no moneybag or knapsack or sandals, did you lack anything?” They said, “Nothing.”
ESV.36: He said to them, “But now let the one who has a moneybag take it, and likewise a knapsack. And let the one who has no sword sell his cloak and buy one.
ESV.37: For I tell you that this Scripture must be fulfilled in me: ‘And he was numbered with the transgressors.’ For what is written about me has its fulfillment.”
ESV.38: And they said, “Look, Lord, here are two swords.” And he said to them,  “It is enough.”
ESV.39: And he came out and went, as was his custom, to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed him.
ESV.40: And when he came to the place, he said to them,  “Pray that you may not enter into temptation.”
ESV.41: And he withdrew from them about a stone's throw, and knelt down and prayed,
ESV.42: saying,  “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”
ESV.43: And there appeared to him an angel from heaven, strengthening him.
ESV.44: And being in agony he prayed more earnestly; and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
ESV.45: And when he rose from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping for sorrow,
ESV.46: and he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”
ESV.47: While he was still speaking, there came a crowd, and the man called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He drew near to Jesus to kiss him,
ESV.48: but Jesus said to him, “Judas, would you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”
ESV.49: And when those who were around him saw what would follow, they said, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”
ESV.50: And one of them struck the servant  of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
ESV.51: But Jesus said, “No more of this!” And he touched his ear and healed him.
ESV.52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests and officers of the temple and elders, who had come out against him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?
ESV.53: When I was with you day after day in the temple, you did not lay hands on me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”
ESV.54: Then they seized him and led him away, bringing him into the high priest's house, and Peter was following at a distance.
ESV.55: And when they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat down among them.
ESV.56: Then a servant girl, seeing him as he sat in the light and looking closely at him, said, “This man also was with him.”
ESV.57: But he denied it, saying, “Woman, I do not know him.”
ESV.58: And a little later someone else saw him and said, “You also are one of them.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not.”
ESV.59: And after an interval of about an hour still another insisted, saying, “Certainly this man also was with him, for he too is a Galilean.”
ESV.60: But Peter said, “Man, I do not know what you are talking about.” And immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
ESV.61: And the Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered the saying of the Lord, how he had said to him,  “Before the rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.”
ESV.62: And he went out and wept bitterly.
ESV.63: Now the men who were holding Jesus in custody were mocking him as they beat him.
ESV.64: They also blindfolded him and kept asking him, “Prophesy! Who is it that struck you?”
ESV.65: And they said many other things against him, blaspheming him.
ESV.66: When day came, the assembly of the elders of the people gathered together, both chief priests and scribes. And they led him away to their council, and they said,
ESV.67: “If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you will not believe,
ESV.68: and if I ask you, you will not answer.
ESV.69: But from now on the Son of Man shall be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
ESV.70: So they all said, “Are you the Son of God, then?” And he said to them,  “You say that I am.”
ESV.71: Then they said, “What further testimony do we need? We have heard it ourselves from his own lips.”

NASB.60: But Peter said, 'Man, I do not know what you are talking about.' Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
NASB.61: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how He had told him, 'Before a rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.'
NASB.62: And he went out and wept bitterly.
NASB.63: Now the men who were holding Jesus in custody were mocking Him and beating Him,
NASB.64: and they blindfolded Him and were asking Him, saying, 'Prophesy, who is the one who hit You?'
NASB.65: And they were saying many other things against Him, blaspheming.
NASB.66: When it was day, the Council of elders of the people assembled, both chief priests and scribes, and they led Him away to their council [chamber], saying,
NASB.67: 'If You are the Christ, tell us.' But He said to them, 'If I tell you, you will not believe;
NASB.68: and if I ask a question, you will not answer.
NASB.69: 'But from now on THE SON OF MAN WILL BE SEATED AT THE RIGHT HAND of the power OF GOD.'
NASB.70: And they all said, 'Are You the Son of God, then?' And He said to them, 'Yes, I am.'
NASB.71: Then they said, 'What further need do we have of testimony? For we have heard it ourselves from His own mouth.'

NLT.1: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is also called Passover, was approaching.
NLT.2: The leading priests and teachers of religious law were plotting how to kill Jesus, but they were afraid of the people’s reaction.
NLT.3: Then Satan entered into Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve disciples,
NLT.4: and he went to the leading priests and captains of the Temple guard to discuss the best way to betray Jesus to them.
NLT.5: They were delighted, and they promised to give him money.
NLT.6: So he agreed and began looking for an opportunity to betray Jesus so they could arrest him when the crowds weren’t around.
NLT.7: Now the Festival of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover lamb is sacrificed.
NLT.8: Jesus sent Peter and John ahead and said, “Go and prepare the Passover meal, so we can eat it together.”
NLT.9: “Where do you want us to prepare it?” they asked him.
NLT.10: He replied, “As soon as you enter Jerusalem, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him. At the house he enters,
NLT.11: say to the owner, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples?’
NLT.12: He will take you upstairs to a large room that is already set up. That is where you should prepare our meal.”
NLT.13: They went off to the city and found everything just as Jesus had said, and they prepared the Passover meal there.
NLT.14: When the time came, Jesus and the apostles sat down together at the table.
NLT.15: Jesus said, “I have been very eager to eat this Passover meal with you before my suffering begins.
NLT.16: For I tell you now that I won’t eat this meal again until its meaning is fulfilled in the Kingdom of God.”
NLT.17: Then he took a cup of wine and gave thanks to God for it. Then he said, “Take this and share it among yourselves.
NLT.18: For I will not drink wine again until the Kingdom of God has come.”
NLT.19: He took some bread and gave thanks to God for it. Then he broke it in pieces and gave it to the disciples, saying, “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
NLT.20: After supper he took another cup of wine and said, “This cup is the new covenant between God and his people — an agreement confirmed with my blood, which is poured out as a sacrifice for you.
NLT.21: “But here at this table, sitting among us as a friend, is the man who will betray me.
NLT.22: For it has been determined that the Son of Man must die. But what sorrow awaits the one who betrays him.”
NLT.23: The disciples began to ask each other which of them would ever do such a thing.
NLT.24: Then they began to argue among themselves about who would be the greatest among them.
NLT.25: Jesus told them, “In this world the kings and great men lord it over their people, yet they are called ‘friends of the people.’
NLT.26: But among you it will be different. Those who are the greatest among you should take the lowest rank, and the leader should be like a servant.
NLT.27: Who is more important, the one who sits at the table or the one who serves? The one who sits at the table, of course. But not here! For I am among you as one who serves.
NLT.28: “You have stayed with me in my time of trial.
NLT.29: And just as my Father has granted me a Kingdom, I now grant you the right
NLT.30: to eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. And you will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NLT.31: “Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift each of you like wheat.
NLT.32: But I have pleaded in prayer for you, Simon, that your faith should not fail. So when you have repented and turned to me again, strengthen your brothers.”
NLT.33: Peter said, “Lord, I am ready to go to prison with you, and even to die with you.”
NLT.34: But Jesus said, “Peter, let me tell you something. Before the rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will deny three times that you even know me.”
NLT.35: Then Jesus asked them, “When I sent you out to preach the Good News and you did not have money, a traveler’s bag, or an extra pair of sandals, did you need anything?” “No,” they replied.
NLT.36: “But now,” he said, “take your money and a traveler’s bag. And if you don’t have a sword, sell your cloak and buy one!
NLT.37: For the time has come for this prophecy about me to be fulfilled: ‘He was counted among the rebels.’ Yes, everything written about me by the prophets will come true.”
NLT.38: “Look, Lord,” they replied, “we have two swords among us.” “That’s enough,” he said.
NLT.39: Then, accompanied by the disciples, Jesus left the upstairs room and went as usual to the Mount of Olives.
NLT.40: There he told them, “Pray that you will not give in to temptation.”
NLT.41: He walked away, about a stone’s throw, and knelt down and prayed,
NLT.42: “Father, if you are willing, please take this cup of suffering away from me. Yet I want your will to be done, not mine.”
NLT.43: Then an angel from heaven appeared and strengthened him.
NLT.44: He prayed more fervently, and he was in such agony of spirit that his sweat fell to the ground like great drops of blood.
NLT.45: At last he stood up again and returned to the disciples, only to find them asleep, exhausted from grief.
NLT.46: “Why are you sleeping?” he asked them. “Get up and pray, so that you will not give in to temptation.”
NLT.47: But even as Jesus said this, a crowd approached, led by Judas, one of the twelve disciples. Judas walked over to Jesus to greet him with a kiss.
NLT.48: But Jesus said, “Judas, would you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”
NLT.49: When the other disciples saw what was about to happen, they exclaimed, “Lord, should we fight? We brought the swords!”
NLT.50: And one of them struck at the high priest’s slave, slashing off his right ear.
NLT.51: But Jesus said, “No more of this.” And he touched the man’s ear and healed him.
NLT.52: Then Jesus spoke to the leading priests, the captains of the Temple guard, and the elders who had come for him. “Am I some dangerous revolutionary,” he asked, “that you come with swords and clubs to arrest me?
NLT.53: Why didn’t you arrest me in the Temple? I was there every day. But this is your moment, the time when the power of darkness reigns.”
NLT.54: So they arrested him and led him to the high priest’s home. And Peter followed at a distance.
NLT.55: The guards lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat around it, and Peter joined them there.
NLT.56: A servant girl noticed him in the firelight and began staring at him. Finally she said, “This man was one of Jesus’ followers!”
NLT.57: But Peter denied it. “Woman,” he said, “I don’t even know him!”
NLT.58: After a while someone else looked at him and said, “You must be one of them!” “No, man, I’m not!” Peter retorted.
NLT.59: About an hour later someone else insisted, “This must be one of them, because he is a Galilean, too.”
NLT.60: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about.” And immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
NLT.61: At that moment the Lord turned and looked at Peter. Suddenly, the Lord’s words flashed through Peter’s mind: “Before the rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will deny three times that you even know me.”
NLT.62: And Peter left the courtyard, weeping bitterly.
NLT.63: The guards in charge of Jesus began mocking and beating him.
NLT.64: They blindfolded him and said, “Prophesy to us! Who hit you that time?”
NLT.65: And they hurled all sorts of terrible insults at him.
NLT.66: At daybreak all the elders of the people assembled, including the leading priests and the teachers of religious law. Jesus was led before this high council,
NLT.67: and they said, “Tell us, are you the Messiah?” But he replied, “If I tell you, you won’t believe me.
NLT.68: And if I ask you a question, you won’t answer.
NLT.69: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated in the place of power at God’s right hand. ”
NLT.70: They all shouted, “So, are you claiming to be the Son of God?” And he replied, “You say that I am.”
NLT.71: “Why do we need other witnesses?” they said. “We ourselves heard him say it.”

WEB.1: Now the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.
WEB.2: The chief priests and the scribes sought how they might put him to death, for they feared the people.
WEB.3: Satan entered into Judas, who was also called Iscariot, who was counted with the twelve.
WEB.4: He went away, and talked with the chief priests and captains about how he might deliver him to them.
WEB.5: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.
WEB.6: He consented, and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the multitude.
WEB.7: The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.
WEB.8: He sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat.”
WEB.9: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare?”
WEB.10: He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house which he enters.
WEB.11: Tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
WEB.12: He will show you a large, furnished upper room. Make preparations there.”
WEB.13: They went, found things as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
WEB.14: When the hour had come, he sat down with the twelve apostles.
WEB.15: He said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,
WEB.16: for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.17: He received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, “Take this, and share it among yourselves,
WEB.18: for I tell you, I will not drink at all again from the fruit of the vine, until God’s Kingdom comes.”
WEB.19: He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke it, and gave to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me.”
WEB.20: Likewise, he took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.
WEB.21: But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.
WEB.22: The Son of Man indeed goes, as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whom he is betrayed!”
WEB.23: They began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.
WEB.24: There arose also a contention among them, which of them was considered to be greatest.
WEB.25: He said to them, “The kings of the nations lord it over them, and those who have authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’
WEB.26: But not so with you. But one who is the greater among you, let him become as the younger, and one who is governing, as one who serves.
WEB.27: For who is greater, one who sits at the table, or one who serves? Isn’t it he who sits at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
WEB.28: But you are those who have continued with me in my trials.
WEB.29: I confer on you a kingdom, even as my Father conferred on me,
WEB.30: that you may eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”
WEB.31: The Lord said, “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have you, that he might sift you as wheat,
WEB.32: but I prayed for you, that your faith wouldn’t fail. You, when once you have turned again, establish your brothers.”
WEB.33: He said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”
WEB.34: He said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will by no means crow today until you deny that you know me three times.”
WEB.35: He said to them, “When I sent you out without purse, and wallet, and shoes, did you lack anything?” They said, “Nothing.”
WEB.36: Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a wallet. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.
WEB.37: For I tell you that this which is written must still be fulfilled in me: ‘He was counted with transgressors.’  For that which concerns me has an end.”
WEB.38: They said, “Lord, behold, here are two swords.” He said to them, “That is enough.”
WEB.39: He came out, and went, as his custom was, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples also followed him.
WEB.40: When he was at the place, he said to them, “Pray that you don’t enter into temptation.”
WEB.41: He was withdrawn from them about a stone’s throw, and he knelt down and prayed,
WEB.42: saying, “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”
WEB.43: An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.
WEB.44: Being in agony he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.
WEB.45: When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples, and found them sleeping because of grief,
WEB.46: and said to them, “Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”
WEB.47: While he was still speaking, behold, a multitude, and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He came near to Jesus to kiss him.
WEB.48: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”
WEB.49: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said to him, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”
WEB.50: A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
WEB.51: But Jesus answered, “Let me at least do this” — and he touched his ear, and healed him.
WEB.52: Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and elders, who had come against him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?
WEB.53: When I was with you in the temple daily, you didn’t stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”
WEB.54: They seized him, and led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter followed from a distance.
WEB.55: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard, and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.
WEB.56: A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, “This man also was with him.”
WEB.57: He denied Jesus, saying, “Woman, I don’t know him.”
WEB.58: After a little while someone else saw him, and said, “You also are one of them!” But Peter answered, “Man, I am not!”
WEB.59: After about one hour passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, “Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean!”
WEB.60: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
WEB.61: The Lord turned, and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the Lord’s word, how he said to him, “Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.”
WEB.62: He went out, and wept bitterly.
WEB.63: The men who held Jesus mocked him and beat him.
WEB.64: Having blindfolded him, they struck him on the face and asked him, “Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?”
WEB.65: They spoke many other things against him, insulting him.
WEB.66: As soon as it was day, the assembly of the elders of the people was gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,
WEB.67: “If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you won’t believe,
WEB.68: and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.
WEB.69: From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
WEB.70: They all said, “Are you then the Son of God?” He said to them, “You say it, because I am.”
WEB.71: They said, “Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!”

AMP.1: Now the Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.
AMP.2: The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to put Him to death; for they were afraid of the people [who listened devotedly to His teaching, and who respected His spiritual wisdom].
AMP.3: Then Satan entered Judas, the one called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve [disciples].
AMP.4: And he went away and discussed with the chief priests and officers how he might betray Him and hand Him over to them.
AMP.5: They were delighted and agreed with him to give him money.
AMP.6: So he consented, and began looking for a good opportunity to betray Jesus to them [at a time when He was] separated from the crowd [because the people might riot or stop them from seizing Him].
AMP.7: Then came the preparation day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
AMP.8: So Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and prepare the Passover meal for us, so that we may eat it."
AMP.9: They asked Him, "Where do You want us to prepare it?"
AMP.10: He replied, "When you have entered the city, a man carrying an earthen jar of water will meet you; follow him into the house that he enters.
AMP.11: And say to the owner of the house, 'The Teacher asks, "Where is the guest room in which I may eat the Passover with My disciples?"'
AMP.12: Then he will show you a large upstairs room, furnished [with carpets and dining couches]; prepare the meal there."
AMP.13: They left and found it just as He had told them; and they prepared the Passover.
AMP.14: When the hour [for the meal] had come, Jesus reclined at the table, and the apostles with Him.
AMP.15: He said to them, "I have earnestly wanted to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;
AMP.16: for I say to you, I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God."
AMP.17: And when He had taken a cup and given thanks, He said, "Take this and share it among yourselves;
AMP.18: for I say to you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine from now on until the kingdom of God comes."
AMP.19: And when He had taken bread and given thanks, He broke it and gave it to them, saying, "This is My body which is given for you; do this in remembrance of Me."
AMP.20: And in the same way He took the cup after they had eaten, saying, "This cup, which is poured out for you, is the new covenant [ratified] in My blood.
AMP.21: But listen, the hand of the one betraying Me is with Mine on the table.
AMP.22: For indeed, the Son of Man is going as it has been determined; but woe (judgment is coming) to that man by whom He is betrayed and handed over!"
AMP.23: And they began to discuss among themselves which one of them it might be who was going to do this.
AMP.24: Now a dispute also arose among them as to which of them was regarded to be the greatest.
AMP.25: Jesus said to them, "The kings of the Gentiles have absolute power and lord it over them; and those in authority over them are called 'Benefactors.'
AMP.26: But it is not to be this way with you; on the contrary, the one who is the greatest among you must become like the youngest [and least privileged], and the [one who is the] leader, like the servant.
AMP.27: For who is the greater, the one who reclines at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who reclines at the table? But I am among you as the one who serves.
AMP.28: "You are those who have remained and have stood by Me in My trials;
AMP.29: and just as My Father has granted Me a kingdom, I grant you [the privilege]
AMP.30: that you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
AMP.31: "Simon, Simon (Peter), listen! Satan has demanded permission to sift [all of] you like grain;
AMP.32: but I have prayed [especially] for you [Peter], that your faith [and confidence in Me] may not fail; and you, once you have turned back again [to Me], strengthen and support your brothers [in the faith]."
AMP.33: And Peter said to Him, "Lord, I am ready to go with You both to prison and to death!"
AMP.34: Jesus said, "I say to you, Peter, before the rooster crows today, you will [utterly] deny three times that you know Me."
AMP.35: And He said to them, "When I sent you out without a money belt and [provision] bag and [extra] sandals, did you lack anything?" They answered, "Nothing."
AMP.36: Then He said to them, "But now, he who has a money belt is to take it along, and also his [provision] bag, and he who has no sword is to sell his coat and buy one.
AMP.37: For I tell you that this [Scripture] which is written must be completed and fulfilled in Me: 'And He was counted with the criminals'; for that which refers to Me has its fulfillment [and is settled]."
AMP.38: They said, "Lord, look, here are two swords." And He said to them, "It is enough."
AMP.39: And He came out and went, as was His habit, to the Mount of Olives; and the disciples followed Him.
AMP.40: When He arrived at the place [called Gethsemane], He said to them, "Pray continually that you may not fall into temptation."
AMP.41: And He withdrew from them about a stone's throw, and knelt down and prayed,
AMP.42: saying, "Father, if You are willing, remove this cup [of divine wrath] from Me; yet not My will, but [always] Yours be done."
AMP.43: Now an angel appeared to Him from heaven, strengthening Him.
AMP.44: And being in agony [deeply distressed and anguished; almost to the point of death], He prayed more intently; and His sweat became like drops of blood, falling down on the ground.
AMP.45: When He rose from prayer, He came to the disciples and found them sleeping from sorrow,
AMP.46: and He said to them, "Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you may not fall into temptation."
AMP.47: While He was still speaking, a crowd came, and the man called Judas, one of the twelve [disciples], was leading the way for them. He approached Jesus to kiss Him.
AMP.48: And Jesus said to him, "Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?"
AMP.49: When those who were around Him saw what was about to happen, they said, "Lord, should we strike with the sword?"
AMP.50: And one of them struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
AMP.51: But Jesus replied, "Stop! No more of this." And He touched the ear and healed him.
AMP.52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests and officers of the temple and elders [of the Sanhedrin] who had come out against Him, "Have you come out with swords and clubs as you would against a robber?
AMP.53: Day after day when I was with you in the temple, you did not lay hands on Me; but this hour and the power and authority of darkness are yours."
AMP.54: Then they seized Him, and led Him away and brought Him to the [elegant] house of the [Jewish] high priest. And Peter was following at a [safe] distance.
AMP.55: After they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.
AMP.56: And a servant-girl, seeing him as he sat in the firelight and looking intently at him, said, "This man was with Him too."
AMP.57: But Peter denied it, saying, "Woman, I do not know Him!"
AMP.58: A little later someone else saw him and said, "You are one of them too." But Peter said, "Man, I am not!"
AMP.59: After about an hour had passed, another man began to insist, "This man was with Him, for he is a Galilean too."
AMP.60: But Peter said, "Man, I do not know what you are talking about." Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
AMP.61: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how He had told him, "Before a rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times."
AMP.62: And he went out and wept bitterly [deeply grieved and distressed].
AMP.63: Now the men who were holding Jesus in custody were mocking and ridiculing Him [and treating Him with contempt] and beating Him.
AMP.64: They blindfolded Him and asked, "Prophesy, who is it that struck You?"
AMP.65: And they were saying many other [evil and slanderous] things against Him, blaspheming [speaking sacrilegiously and abusively about] Him.
AMP.66: When day came, the Council of the elders of the people (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court) assembled, both chief priests and scribes; and they led Jesus away to the council chamber, saying,
AMP.67: "If You are the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), tell us." But He said to them, "If I tell you, you will not believe [what I say],
AMP.68: and if I ask a question, you will not answer.
AMP.69: But from now on, <e>the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand</e> of the power <e>of God</e>."
AMP.70: And they all said, "Are You the Son of God, then?" He replied, "It is just as you say."
AMP.71: Then they asked, "What further need of testimony do we have? For we ourselves have heard it from His own mouth."

NRSVCE.44: In his anguish he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.]]
NRSVCE.45: When he got up from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping because of grief,
NRSVCE.46: and he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you may not come into the time of trial.”
NRSVCE.47: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd came, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him;
NRSVCE.48: but Jesus said to him, “Judas, is it with a kiss that you are betraying the Son of Man?”
NRSVCE.49: When those who were around him saw what was coming, they asked, “Lord, should we strike with the sword?”
NRSVCE.50: Then one of them struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
NRSVCE.51: But Jesus said, “No more of this!” And he touched his ear and healed him.
NRSVCE.52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple police, and the elders who had come for him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a bandit?
NRSVCE.53: When I was with you day after day in the temple, you did not lay hands on me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness!”
NRSVCE.54: Then they seized him and led him away, bringing him into the high priest’s house. But Peter was following at a distance.
NRSVCE.55: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
NRSVCE.56: Then a servant-girl, seeing him in the firelight, stared at him and said, “This man also was with him.”
NRSVCE.57: But he denied it, saying, “Woman, I do not know him.”
NRSVCE.58: A little later someone else, on seeing him, said, “You also are one of them.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
NRSVCE.59: Then about an hour later still another kept insisting, “Surely this man also was with him; for he is a Galilean.”
NRSVCE.60: But Peter said, “Man, I do not know what you are talking about!” At that moment, while he was still speaking, the cock crowed.
NRSVCE.61: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before the cock crows today, you will deny me three times.”
NRSVCE.62: And he went out and wept bitterly.
NRSVCE.63: Now the men who were holding Jesus began to mock him and beat him;
NRSVCE.64: they also blindfolded him and kept asking him, “Prophesy! Who is it that struck you?”
NRSVCE.65: They kept heaping many other insults on him.
NRSVCE.66: When day came, the assembly of the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, gathered together, and they brought him to their council.
NRSVCE.67: They said, “If you are the Messiah, tell us.” He replied, “If I tell you, you will not believe;
NRSVCE.68: and if I question you, you will not answer.
NRSVCE.69: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
NRSVCE.70: All of them asked, “Are you, then, the Son of God?” He said to them, “You say that I am.”
NRSVCE.71: Then they said, “What further testimony do we need? We have heard it ourselves from his own lips!”

NET.1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.
NET.2: The chief priests and the experts in the law were trying to find some way to execute Jesus, for they were afraid of the people.
NET.3: Then Satan entered Judas, the one called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve.
NET.4: He went away and discussed with the chief priests and officers of the temple guard how he might betray Jesus, handing him over to them.
NET.5: They were delighted and arranged to give him money.
NET.6: So Judas agreed and began looking for an opportunity to betray Jesus when no crowd was present.
NET.7: Then the day for the feast of Unleavened Bread came, on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
NET.8: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us to eat.”
NET.9: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”
NET.10: He said to them, “Listen, when you have entered the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him into the house that he enters,
NET.11: and tell the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
NET.12: Then he will show you a large furnished room upstairs. Make preparations there.”
NET.13: So they went and found things just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
NET.14: Now when the hour came, Jesus took his place at the table and the apostles joined him.
NET.15: And he said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
NET.16: For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
NET.17: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves.
NET.18: For I tell you that from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
NET.19: Then he took bread, and after giving thanks he broke it and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
NET.20: And in the same way he took the cup after they had eaten, saying, “This cup that is poured out for you is the new covenant in my blood.
NET.21: “But look, the hand of the one who betrays me is with me on the table.
NET.22: For the Son of Man is to go just as it has been determined, but woe to that man by whom he is betrayed!”
NET.23: So they began to question one another as to which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
NET.24: A dispute also started among them over which of them was to be regarded as the greatest.
NET.25: So Jesus said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’
NET.26: Not so with you; instead the one who is greatest among you must become like the youngest, and the leader like the one who serves.
NET.27: For who is greater, the one who is seated at the table, or the one who serves? Is it not the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
NET.28: “You are the ones who have remained with me in my trials.
NET.29: Thus I grant to you a kingdom, just as my Father granted to me,
NET.30: that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NET.31: “Simon, Simon, pay attention! Satan has demanded to have you all, to sift you like wheat,
NET.32: but I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. When you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.”
NET.33: But Peter said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”
NET.34: Jesus replied, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know me.”
NET.35: Then Jesus said to them, “When I sent you out with no money bag, or traveler’s bag, or sandals, you didn’t lack anything, did you?” They replied, “Nothing.”
NET.36: He said to them, “But now, the one who has a money bag must take it, and likewise a traveler’s bag too. And the one who has no sword must sell his cloak and buy one.
NET.37: For I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in me, ‘And he was counted with the transgressors.’ For what is written about me is being fulfilled.”
NET.38: So they said, “Look, Lord, here are two swords.” Then he told them, “It is enough.”
NET.39: Then Jesus went out and made his way, as he customarily did, to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed him.
NET.40: When he came to the place, he said to them, “Pray that you will not fall into temptation.”
NET.41: He went away from them about a stone’s throw, knelt down, and prayed,
NET.42: “Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me. Yet not my will but yours be done.” [
NET.43: Then an angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him.
NET.44: And in his anguish he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat was like drops of blood falling to the ground.]
NET.45: When he got up from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping, exhausted from grief.
NET.46: So he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you will not fall into temptation!”
NET.47: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd appeared, and the man named Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He walked up to Jesus to kiss him.
NET.48: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, would you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”
NET.49: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said, “Lord, should we use our swords?”
NET.50: Then one of them struck the high priest’s slave, cutting off his right ear.
NET.51: But Jesus said, “Enough of this!” And he touched the man’s ear and healed him.
NET.52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come out to get him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs like you would against an outlaw?
NET.53: Day after day when I was with you in the temple courts, you did not arrest me. But this is your hour, and that of the power of darkness!”
NET.54: Then they arrested Jesus, led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter was following at a distance.
NET.55: When they had made a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat down among them.
NET.56: Then a slave girl, seeing him as he sat in the firelight, stared at him and said, “This man was with him too!”
NET.57: But Peter denied it: “Woman, I don’t know him!”
NET.58: Then a little later someone else saw him and said, “You are one of them too.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
NET.59: And after about an hour still another insisted, “Certainly this man was with him, because he too is a Galilean.”
NET.60: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you’re talking about!” At that moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
NET.61: Then the Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.”
NET.62: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
NET.63: Now the men who were holding Jesus under guard began to mock him and beat him.
NET.64: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, “Prophesy! Who hit you?”
NET.65: They also said many other things against him, reviling him.
NET.66: When day came, the council of the elders of the people gathered together, both the chief priests and the experts in the law. Then they led Jesus away to their council
NET.67: and said, “If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you will not believe,
NET.68: and if I ask you, you will not answer.
NET.69: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
NET.70: So they all said, “Are you the Son of God, then?” He answered them, “You say that I am.”
NET.71: Then they said, “Why do we need further testimony? We have heard it ourselves from his own lips!”
